about.intro: I'm a *Backend & Game Developer*, graduated with **Honors in DAM** and passionate about **interactive experiences**.
about.description: Multiplatform developer specialized in video game environments (Unity). No. 1 of my class in DAM (GPA: 9.36) and current Game Design and Development student at URJC. I combine a solid systems and backend logical foundation with technical creation in game engines.
about.full:
Hi, I'm **Hugo Mazarío Ros**. My passion for computer science led me to graduate as a Higher Technical Degree in Multiplatform Application Development (DAM) at The Core School, achieving the **No. 1 spot of my graduating class and obtaining full academic Honors**.

I have practical experience in game development, having worked professionally as an **Apprentice Unity Developer** at 'Proyecto Moción - Comunidad de Jesús', where I took charge of version control under GitLab and designed the game's most complex logical systems and puzzles.

Currently, I continue to expand my horizons by studying Game Design and Development at URJC. I master the *C#* language and the *Unity* engine, applying development best practices to build smooth mechanics and optimally integrate 3D assets designed in tools like *3ds Max*. I am a proactive, detail-oriented person in constant technical evolution.

projects.title: My Neighbor Totoro (AGS)
projects.description: A faithful recreation of a classic graphic adventure inspired by the Studio Ghibli universe. Built entirely using Adventure Game Studio (AGS), where I programmed interaction logic, character movement scripts, and managed dynamic dialogues and cutscenes.

projects.title: Landing Page Path To Nowhere
projects.description: An interactive web project focused on high-fidelity frontend design and development. Built with pure web technologies, implementing responsive layouts, browser-optimized animations, and clean code to ensure a fluid and reactive user experience across devices.

projects.title: Eiden's Rebirth
projects.description: Playable 2D game demo developed in Unity and published on Itch.io. An active, continuously expanding project that served as my Final Degree Project (TFG) for my DAM degree, integrating complex gameplay mechanics, physics, and visual polish.

roadmap.description: Higher Technical Degree in DAM (No. 1 of Class, 9.36 GPA, full academic Honors). Specialization in programming logic, database systems, and multiplatform development. Published 'Eiden's Rebirth' as my final thesis project.
Apprentice Unity Developer at Proyecto Moción. Developed advanced puzzle mechanics, took technical charge of Git/GitLab workflows, and coordinated within an interactive software development team.
Bachelor's Degree in Game Design and Development (URJC). Deepening my skills in high-performance gameplay mechanics, art pipelines, low-poly modeling, and building interactive projects.
